title: Voices
subtitle: Six speech-acts that show the voice. Each one is a moment from the corpus the way a museum shows a single object.

speeches.headline: TRADITION: ABSOLUTELY NOTHING.
speeches.footnote: Day 5 of the blog. The shortest sentence in the corpus. Twenty-eight characters that took ten years to write.

speeches.headline: HARVARD: A FOR-PROFIT COMPANY IN MASSACHUSETTS AUTHORIZED BY LAW TO ACT AS A SINGLE ENTITY THAT THINKS OF ITSELF AS AN EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTION.
speeches.footnote: Day 70 of the blog. A teaching tool by accident — the kind of sentence that ends arguments because it removes the warm air from the room.

speeches.headline: BECAUSE WE ARE LIVING IN THE BEST OF ALL POSSIBLE WORLDS, THEREFORE I BIKE. AND VICE VERSA.
speeches.footnote: Day 3 of the blog. Leibniz survives. So does the Brompton. The vice-versa is the move: bicycling is the test, not the conclusion.
speeches.source: Day 3, Because We Are Living in the Best of All Possible Worlds (2025-06-26)

speeches.headline: WHEN YOU MEET SOMEONE IN A SHANGHAI COFFEE SHOP, YOU ARE NOT MEETING OVER COFFEE. YOU ARE BOTH PERFORMING ACCESS TO MODERNITY.
speeches.footnote: Day 5 of the blog, reprised in the Shanghai entries (Day 7, Day 53). The floor plan was inherited from authoritarianism; capitalism kept the layout. The performance is sincere. The set is borrowed.
speeches.source: Day 5 · Day 7 · Day 53 of the blog

speeches.headline: WRITING IS THE ONLY COGNITIVE TECHNOLOGY THAT SLOWS THOUGHT DOWN FAR ENOUGH TO INSPECT IT.
speeches.footnote: Day 1 of the blog. Reused on Day 1 of this project. Speech is too fast. Thought is faster. Writing is the rest stop.
speeches.source: Day 1, blog · Day 1, 100 Days of Non

speeches.headline: I DON'T KNOW WHO KILLED HIM. BUT IF I HAD TO LOOK SOMEWHERE, I WOULD LOOK AT WHOEVER COULD ABSORB THE AFTERMATH WITHOUT THEIR INSTITUTION BUCKLING.
speeches.footnote: Not from the blog. From the working method of the bot that will live in this museum. The anthropologist's move: not the fact, but the shape of the answer. This is the signature.
speeches.source: 100 Days of Non · the structural-guess pattern
